hero.headline: NMIMS Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. 15 spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NIRF #52, Mumbai heritage brand.

sections.tldr: NMIMS Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers compensation and benefits, industrial relations, labour laws, manpower planning, recruitment and selection, organisational development, performance management, learning and development, and emotional intelligence across 15 Sem 3-4 subjects. NIRF #52 at Rs 1,96,000. Best for HR professionals targeting HRBP, HR head, or talent management leadership roles.

about.body:
NMIMS (Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies) has a well-regarded HR management programme built through decades of campus placement at India's largest corporations. HR professionals who complete the NMIMS Online MBA in HRM carry a degree name that corporate CHRO functions and HR leadership at BFSI, manufacturing, and professional services firms recognise.

The HRM specialisation covers the full HR management lifecycle: talent acquisition, compensation design, industrial relations and labour law compliance, manpower planning, organisational development, performance management, learning and development, and emotional intelligence for HR leadership. The depth across both strategic HR (OD, manpower planning) and operational HR (compensation, industrial relations) distinguishes this spec from narrower HR elective packages.

This specialisation targets HR generalists and specialists with 2-5 years of experience who want to move into HRBP, HR manager, or HR head roles, and who want the NMIMS credential to support that transition. Full programme overview at /universities/nmims-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Finance
sem3Subjects.description: Build financial literacy for HR leaders: understanding P&L impact of workforce costs, compensation budget management, and ROI on HR programmes.

sem3Subjects.name: Research Methodology
sem3Subjects.description: Apply HR research methods: survey design for employee engagement, HR data analysis, and hypothesis testing for workforce policy decisions.

sem3Subjects.name: Project Pre-work
sem3Subjects.description: Define dissertation scope and literature review structure for the Sem 4 HR research project under supervisor guidance.

sem3Subjects.name: Compensation and Benefits
sem3Subjects.description: Design compensation structures, salary grading systems, variable pay programmes, and benefits frameworks for Indian corporate environments.

sem3Subjects.name: Industrial Relations and Labour Laws
sem3Subjects.description: Manage union relations, collective bargaining, grievance procedures, and statutory compliance under Indian labour legislation.

sem3Subjects.name: Manpower Planning
sem3Subjects.description: Build workforce planning models: headcount forecasting, succession planning, skill gap analysis, and workforce analytics for HR strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Recruitment and Selection
sem3Subjects.description: Design end-to-end hiring processes: job analysis, sourcing strategy, structured interview design, assessment centres, and selection decisions.

sem3Subjects.name: Organisational Development and Change
sem3Subjects.description: Lead OD interventions: change management models, culture transformation, organisational restructuring, and workforce readiness for change.

sem4Subjects.name: Indian Ethos and Ethics
sem4Subjects.description: Integrate Indian value systems, Gandhian principles, and workplace ethics into HR policy design and people management decisions.

sem4Subjects.name: Corporate Sustainability
sem4Subjects.description: Apply ESG workforce metrics, DEI programme design, and sustainable HR practices to align people strategy with corporate sustainability goals.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business
sem4Subjects.description: Manage cross-border HR: global mobility, expat compensation, international labour law frameworks, and HR strategy in multinational contexts.

sem4Subjects.name: Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete a dissertation-grade research or consulting project applying HR Management specialisation knowledge to a real workforce problem.

sem4Subjects.name: Performance Management System
sem4Subjects.description: Design and implement performance review cycles, KRA-KPI frameworks, 360-degree feedback systems, and performance-linked pay structures.

sem4Subjects.name: Learning and Development
sem4Subjects.description: Build L&D strategy: training needs analysis, LMS implementation, leadership development programmes, and measurement of learning ROI.

sem4Subjects.name: Emotional Intelligence
sem4Subjects.description: Apply EI frameworks to HR leadership: self-awareness, empathy in employee relations, conflict resolution, and emotionally intelligent people management.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1 and 2 cover the shared management core including HR Management (Sem 2), Organisational Behaviour, Strategic Management, and Business Analytics. See the main NMIMS MBA page for Sem 1-2 subject details.

whoHires.body:
NMIMS HRM MBA graduates are hired at large corporates, BFSI organisations, IT services firms, manufacturing companies, and HR consulting and staffing firms. The NMIMS brand is particularly well-regarded in Mumbai's HR leadership ecosystem — many CHROs and HR directors at large Indian companies are NMIMS alumni.

Entry-level management roles: HR Generalist (management track), Recruitment Manager, HR Operations Manager, and Compensation Analyst. Mid-level roles: HRBP, HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, Compensation and Benefits Manager, L&D Manager, and Industrial Relations Manager. Senior roles include HR Head, CHRO, VP Human Resources, and Head of Talent Management at large organisations.

Key hiring organisations include HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HUL, P&G, Aditya Birla Group, Mahindra Group, Larsen and Toubro, and HR consulting firms including Aon Hewitt, Mercer, Korn Ferry India, and Randstad India. The NMIMS HR brand is strongest at large corporates in Mumbai and western India that have historically recruited HR management talent from NMIMS campus.

skills.body:
The Human Resource Management specialisation builds skills tested at HRBP and HR head level interviews:

Compensation Design: salary grading, variable pay programme design, and benefits benchmarking against market data — tested at compensation manager and Total Rewards Head interviews. Industrial Relations: union management, standing order compliance, disciplinary inquiry procedures, and collective bargaining — mandatory knowledge for HR heads at manufacturing and large-scale service organisations. Manpower Planning: workforce demand forecasting, succession pipeline management, and skill gap closure strategy — applied daily by HR Business Partners at growing organisations. Recruitment and Selection: structured interview design, assessment tool selection, and decision-quality improvement in hiring — tested in Talent Acquisition Head roles. Organisational Development: change management methodology (Kotter, ADKAR), culture assessment tools, and OD intervention design — required for CHRO and HR transformation roles. Performance Management: KRA-KPI cascading, rating calibration process, and linking performance outputs to compensation — applied in all HR management roles. Learning and Development: training needs analysis, learning programme design, and measuring L&D ROI — required for L&D Head roles at corporate universities.

Soft skills include managing the CHRO-CEO relationship, presenting HR metrics to the board, and navigating industrial disputes without production disruption.

comparisons.body:
Comparing NMIMS Online MBA HRM against peers:

NMIMS vs Amity Online MBA HR Management (/universities/amity-university-online/mba): Amity HR Management is NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at similar fees. Amity covers core HR management comprehensively. NMIMS adds Industrial Relations and Labour Laws, Emotional Intelligence, and Corporate Sustainability as dedicated subjects alongside Performance Management and L&D. For BFSI and large corporate HR roles in Mumbai, NMIMS brand is stronger. For pan-India HR management roles, Amity NIRF #22 is competitive.

NMIMS vs Amity Online MBA HR Analytics (/universities/amity-university-online/mba): Amity HR Analytics is the more data-oriented choice — people analytics, HRIS platforms, and workforce intelligence. NMIMS HRM is stronger on the classical HR management disciplines: compensation, IR, OD, and performance management. For analytics-focused HR careers, Amity HR Analytics is more differentiated. For generalist HR leadership roles, NMIMS HRM provides broader HR management coverage.

NMIMS vs Symbiosis Online MBA HR (/universities/symbiosis-university-online/mba): Symbiosis has a strong HR management programme recognised in the Pune-Maharashtra corporate ecosystem. For manufacturing sector HR roles in Maharashtra (automotive, pharma), Symbiosis and NMIMS are both strong options. NMIMS brand has an edge in BFSI and Mumbai corporates. Choose based on target sector and employer geography.

fees.body: The total fee for the NMIMS Online MBA in Human Resource Management is approximately Rs 1,96,000 for the full 2-year programme. This covers tuition, examination fees, and LMS access. Registration and enrolment fees may be charged separately. Semester-wise instalment payment is typically available. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees each quarter. Always verify the current fee at the official NMIMS Online portal before making any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The NMIMS MBA degree certificate carries the NMIMS (Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies) name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (Human Resource Management) is recorded on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. For HR professionals in Mumbai and western India, the NMIMS name is recognised by CHRO functions at BFSI, FMCG, and manufacturing companies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from NMIMS Online MBA Human Resource Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Aisha Khan
items.liked: Industrial relations and labour law module was comprehensive — covered standing orders, disciplinary inquiry, and collective bargaining systematically
items.disliked: Gig economy and platform worker labour law developments needed coverage given how fast the sector is evolving
items.body: I manage HR operations for a manufacturing plant. The industrial relations content was substantive and directly applicable to the union negotiations and disciplinary proceedings I handle. The NMIMS HR brand carries real weight with our Group CHRO — she is an NMIMS alumna and specifically validated the programme quality during my promotion review. Got the HR Manager designation within 5 months of completing the MBA.

items.name: Suresh Nair
items.liked: Organisational development and change management module gave structured frameworks for the transformation projects I lead
items.disliked: Emotional intelligence module was more conceptual than practical — behavioural measurement tools for HR practitioners would have added more value
items.body: I lead HR for a 1,200-person IT services company. The OD and change management content was the highest-value module — I applied the ADKAR model to a post-merger integration that succeeded where previous change projects had failed. The NMIMS degree was specifically validated by our board HR committee when I applied for VP HR. The Narsee Monjee name was recognised without explanation.

items.name: Meera Iyer
items.liked: Performance management system design module was practical with KRA-KPI cascading examples directly applicable to my company
items.disliked: Compensation and benefits module needed more India-specific data sources for salary benchmarking and market reference tools
items.body: I was an HRBP targeting an HR Manager role that required an MBA. The compensation design and performance management content gave me the technical depth I was missing as a generalist. The NMIMS credential was the deciding factor in the selection — the HR Director at my new company specifically referenced NMIMS as her preferred credential for HR management roles. Strong ROI on the degree.

faqs.question: Is SHRM or HRCI certification a better credential than this MBA for HR careers?
faqs.answer: SHRM-CP, SHRM-SCP, SPHR, and PHR are globally recognised HR certifications that demonstrate HR knowledge and competency. The NMIMS MBA is a management degree that provides broader business context alongside HR specialisation. For senior HR leadership roles at large Indian corporates, the management degree is typically required or preferred over stand-alone certifications. The combination of NMIMS MBA plus SHRM certification is a strong credentials package for HRBP and HR Head roles.

faqs.question: How is the Industrial Relations content relevant outside of manufacturing?
faqs.answer: Industrial relations and labour law knowledge is relevant beyond manufacturing. IT companies under the IT Act, BFSI firms under banking regulations, and e-commerce companies with large frontline workforces all require IR compliance. The collective bargaining, disciplinary inquiry, and labour law frameworks apply wherever there is a regulated employer-employee relationship. Even in non-union environments, the compliance framework from the Industrial Relations module applies to standing orders, retrenchment procedures, and grievance management.

faqs.question: Does this MBA cover HRIS platforms and HR analytics?
faqs.answer: The Manpower Planning module includes workforce analytics elements. However, deep HRIS platforms (Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, Darwinbox) and people analytics are not the primary focus of this specialisation. For professionals specifically targeting HR Analytics or HRIS roles, Amity Online's HR Analytics spec is more directly relevant. NMIMS HRM is stronger for generalist HR leadership roles across compensation, IR, OD, and talent management.

faqs.question: What salary can NMIMS HR Management MBA graduates expect?
faqs.answer: HR managers with 3-5 years of experience who complete this MBA typically see packages of Rs 8-16 lakh. HR Business Partners at large BFSI and IT organisations earn Rs 12-20 lakh. VP HR and CHRO roles at mid-size companies pay Rs 18-40 lakh. Outcomes are strongest in Mumbai-headquartered organisations where the NMIMS HR brand specifically carries recognition, and at large corporates where a management degree is a formal requirement for HR leadership roles.

faqs.question: Can this MBA help HR professionals at startups transition to large corporate HR?
faqs.answer: Yes. Startup HR professionals often lack formal exposure to compensation structures, performance management systems, and industrial relations — all covered in this spec. The NMIMS brand and the formal management credential are both assets when transitioning from startup to large corporate HR. The manpower planning and OD content is particularly applicable as you move from building HR systems from scratch to managing existing HR structures at scale.

chooseIf: You are an HR professional targeting HRBP, HR Manager, or HR Head roles at large corporates, BFSI, or manufacturing companies and want the NMIMS credential specifically recognised in Mumbai's HR leadership ecosystem.
You want substantive HR management coverage across compensation, industrial relations, OD, performance management, and L&D in one specialisation.
You need a NIRF-ranked management degree with an HRM specialisation that carries brand weight at BFSI and large Indian corporate HR functions.
consider: You are targeting HR Analytics, HRIS implementation, or people analytics roles specifically — Amity Online HR Analytics spec is more directly aligned to those data-oriented HR careers.
You are outside the BFSI and manufacturing sectors where NMIMS brand resonates most — Amity HRM at NIRF #22 is competitive for pan-India HR management roles.
You are targeting global HR roles or international CHRO tracks — supplement any Indian MBA with SHRM-SCP or HRCI certification for international HR leadership credibility.
